question.text: Using the interest rate parity formula, if EURJPY spot is 123.40, JPY 1-year rate is 0.25%, and EUR 1-year rate is 1%, what is the 1-year forward rate?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** F = S × (1+RJPY)/(1+REUR) = 123.40 × (1.0025)/(1.01) = 123.40 × 0.99257 = 122.4837 ≈ 122.48. Since EUR has a higher rate, EUR is at a forward discount to JPY. **The Trap:** JPY is the quote currency here. The forward rate is LOWER than spot because EUR (base) has higher interest. **Cross-Exam Context:** Cross-currency pricing is tested in Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** EUR at forward discount to JPY means you get fewer JPY per EUR in the future — EUR weakens against JPY in forward markets.
